selectedEmail.title: Request for Guidance on iObeya API Setup for Python-Based Data Extraction

jiraDraft.summary: Request for Guidance on iObeya API Setup for Python-Based Data Extraction
jiraDraft.description:
Hi Team,

We are currently working on integrating with iObeya (version 4.20) APIs, with the primary objective of fetching data (rooms, boards, and board content) using Python-based automation scripts.

At present, we have created an OAuth application; however, we observe that only the following grant types are available:
- Authorization Code
- Refresh Token
- Implicit Authorization

Our requirement is to enable non-interactive, server-to-server API access, as our use case involves backend Python scripts and scheduled jobs (no browser or user login interaction).

We would like your guidance on the following:
- Whether iObeya supports the OAuth2 client_credentials grant type in our current setup (version 4.20).

- If supported:
- How to enable this grant type for our tenant
- Steps to create an API client and obtain client_id and client_secret

- If not supported:
- What is the recommended approach for securely accessing APIs via Python scripts?
- Is there any alternative authentication mechanism suitable for automation use cases?

- Any official documentation or examples for:
- Authenticating and generating access tokens
- Fetching rooms, boards, and widgets via API
- Best practices for token handling in automated scripts

Our goal is to build a reliable and secure integration where Python scripts can periodically fetch and process iObeya data without manual intervention.

We want to ensure that the implementation aligns with iObeya’s supported and recommended approach.

I can see below docs related to API.

https://doc.api.iobeya.io/#section/How-to-get-access-to-the-iObeya-API/Getting-started
https://center.iobeya.com/integration-articles/machine-to-machine-requests-to-the-apis/

Looking forward to your guidance.

Thanks & Regards,
Manojkumar M
clientOptions: Agence du numérique de la sécurité civile
N+P SUBCOAL PRODUCTION FRM B.V. via EMS COGNITO
Rolls Royce Civil Aerospace Derby via Project 7
SG ABS ( Société Générale African Business Services)
SIAE - Atelier Industriel de l’Aéronautique AIA

Un sous-cas 'Intervention administration' peut émerger si le client confirme qu'il faut activer/configurer le grant type côté tenant.

selectedEmail.title: New Support Request - Eli Lilly - USA -  Ireland
Following your support request - ABB S.p.A. -  Italy

jiraDraft.summary: Following your support request - ABB S.p.A. -  Italy
jiraDraft.description:
Hello,

You contacted us with the following message :
I've updated our TEST environment to latest 4.46.7 on-prem version, but I'm not able to get iObeya running correctly. I've update also Java OpenJDK to 21.0.11.10.1, Apache Tomcat to 9.0.118 and MySQL to 8.0.46: I'm not able to update MySQL to 8.4.9 ... Moreover, also the PROD environment with 4.43.8 is not working anymore: I've attached the files inside logs folder. Could you kindly help to solve both issues? Thank you very much.

From what I see in the logs, when iObeya is starting, there is an error about reaching the database :
Caused by: com.mysql.cj.exceptions.CJCommunicationsException: Communications link failure
The last packet sent successfully to the server was 0 milliseconds ago. The driver has not received any packets from the server.
[...]
Caused by: java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused: connect

Since you updated a lot of requirements, can you check that everything is set up correctly please . For example, the JDBC driver is the correct version and inside the Tomcat/lib directory. In the ROOT.xml file, did you set up the mysql connection and credentials correctly ? Etc.

After checking everything, please restart your Tomcat and send us the logs again. We only need the iObeya logs for the day (it is not necessary to send us your logs from 2024) and the Tomcat logs please.

As for the production environment that is not working anymore : did you change anything ? Did you try to restart your server ? And we need the Tomcat and iObeya logs (only for today).

Thank you,
clientOptions: Agence du numérique de la sécurité civile
N+P SUBCOAL PRODUCTION FRM B.V. via EMS COGNITO
Rolls Royce Civil Aerospace Derby via Project 7
SG ABS ( Société Générale African Business Services)
SIAE - Atelier Industriel de l’Aéronautique AIA

identificationWarnings: Demande de confirmation sur une limitation produit, sans action administrative ni licence explicite.
